cockpit-cms.name: Cockpit CMS
cockpit-cms.short_description: Self-hosted, MIT-licensed headless CMS with REST/GraphQL APIs, flexible content models, and optional paid Pro addons.

features.description: REST and GraphQL APIs for querying content, compatible with Next.js, React, Vue, Svelte, PHP, and Node.js.
features.title: API-first architecture

features.description: Collections, Singletons, and Trees with custom and conditional fields for structuring content.
features.title: Flexible content models

features.description: Runs on your own server (PHP 7.3+, SQLite or MongoDB) with no vendor lock-in and no required account.
features.title: Self-hosted deployment

features.description: Built-in multi-language content and key-based language strings, with optional AI-assisted translation via DeepL or LibreTranslate.
features.title: Localization

features.description: Cloud storage support, nestable asset folders, automatic color extraction, and an image API for thumbnail generation.
features.title: Asset management

features.description: Fine-grained access control for multiple user roles.
features.title: Role-based permissions

features.description: Ability to revert content to previous versions.
features.title: Content revisions

features.description: Optional paid add-ons including Autopilot (AI assistant), Detektivo (Meilisearch full-text search), Sync (environment deployment), and Layouts & Pages (visual page builder).
features.title: Pro addons

pros: MIT-licensed open-source core that is free forever, with no required account or vendor lock-in.
Fully self-hosted, giving organizations complete control over their data.
Supports both REST and GraphQL APIs, usable with a wide range of frontend and backend stacks.
One-time Pro license (€299) instead of a mandatory recurring subscription.
Flexible content modeling with Collections, Singletons, and Trees.
cons: Advanced features (AI assistant, full-text search, visual page builder, environment sync) require the paid Pro add-on license.
Self-hosting requires managing your own PHP server environment (PHP 7.3+, SQLite/MongoDB, Apache with mod_rewrite).
Pro license renewal (€99/year) is needed to keep receiving updates after the first year.
Smaller GitHub community than some competitors, with under 6k stars on the legacy agentejo/cockpit repository.

faqs.answer: Yes, the Cockpit core is MIT-licensed and free forever; a paid Pro tier (€299 one-time per project, €99/year for continued updates) unlocks additional addons, and custom Enterprise pricing is also available.
faqs.question: Is Cockpit CMS free?

faqs.answer: Yes, Cockpit is designed to be installed on your own server and requires PHP 7.3+, PDO with SQLite or MongoDB, and Apache with mod_rewrite.
faqs.question: Is Cockpit CMS self-hosted?

faqs.answer: Yes, Cockpit offers both REST and GraphQL APIs for content access.
faqs.question: Does Cockpit CMS support GraphQL?

faqs.answer: Paid Pro addons include Autopilot (AI content and image generation), Detektivo (Meilisearch-powered full-text search), Sync (environment-to-environment deployment), Layouts & Pages (visual page building with SEO tools), and Inbox (form submissions).
faqs.question: What are Cockpit's Pro addons?

faqs.answer: Cockpit is made by Agentejo, with current development continuing under the Cockpit-HQ organization on GitHub.
faqs.question: Who makes Cockpit CMS?

features: All Pro addons (Autopilot, Detektivo, Sync, Layouts & Pages, Inbox)
Covers one production instance plus unlimited dev/staging environments

silverstripe-cms.name: Silverstripe CMS
silverstripe-cms.short_description: Silverstripe CMS is a free, open-source BSD-licensed PHP CMS and framework, with 50,000+ live installations and optional paid Cloud hosting.

features.description: Silverstripe CMS is free to self-host, distributed under the permissive BSD license.
features.title: Open-source core under the BSD license

features.description: A dedicated templating engine handles frontend rendering and theming.
features.title: Templating engine

features.description: Role-based access management lets teams restrict editing and publishing rights.
features.title: Granular permission controls

features.description: The community has published more than 2,500 modules extending core functionality.
features.title: 2,500+ modules

features.description: A quote-based managed hosting platform, ISO/IEC 27001 certified, run by commercial backer Silverstripe Limited.
features.title: Silverstripe Cloud

features.description: The platform is built to serve four groups: developers, marketers, content authors, and IT managers.
features.title: Multi-audience design

pros: Free and open source under the permissive BSD license
Large install base, with 50,000+ live Silverstripe CMS sites reported by silverstripe.org
2,500+ community modules extend core functionality
Backed by commercial company Silverstripe Limited, offering support and a certified Cloud hosting platform
Used by notable organizations including Lenovo, Westpac, and Lord's Cricket Ground
cons: Silverstripe Cloud pricing is not publicly listed and requires contacting sales for a quote
Smaller module ecosystem than larger CMS platforms like WordPress or Drupal
Requires PHP development expertise to customize beyond out-of-the-box functionality

faqs.answer: Yes, the core CMS is free and open source, distributed under the BSD License.
faqs.question: Is Silverstripe CMS free?

faqs.answer: Silverstripe was founded in 2000 in Wellington, New Zealand, by Sam Minnée, Sigurd Magnusson, and Tim Copeland.
faqs.question: Who created Silverstripe?

faqs.answer: Silverstripe.org reports over 50,000 live Silverstripe CMS installations.
faqs.question: How many sites run on Silverstripe?

faqs.answer: Yes, Silverstripe Cloud offers managed, scalable hosting, but pricing is not publicly listed and requires contacting sales.
faqs.question: Is there managed hosting for Silverstripe CMS?

faqs.answer: The BSD License.
faqs.question: What license is Silverstripe CMS released under?

backdrop-cms.name: Backdrop CMS
backdrop-cms.short_description: Backdrop CMS is a free, open-source PHP CMS (GPL v2) forked from Drupal 7 in 2013, built for budget-conscious websites.

features.description: Backdrop CMS was forked from Drupal 7 in 2013, retaining a similar architecture and offering a built-in upgrade path from Drupal 7 sites.
features.title: Drupal 7 fork

features.description: Add-ons and themes can be browsed and installed directly from the Backdrop CMS site.
features.title: 1,300+ modules and add-ons

features.description: Backdrop is designed for fast page performance even on shared/budget hosting.
features.title: Runs on modest hosting

features.description: Content can be created and managed without programming knowledge.
features.title: No-code content creation

features.description: The project is guided by a Project Management Committee (PMC), modeled on the Apache Software Foundation's governance approach.
features.title: Community governance

features.description: Backdrop CMS is a member project of the Software Freedom Conservancy, a nonprofit that supports open-source projects.
features.title: Software Freedom Conservancy member

pros: Completely free, open source (GPL v2 or later), with no paid tiers or licensing fees
Straightforward upgrade path for existing Drupal 7 sites
Runs well on modest or shared hosting
1,300+ community modules and add-ons available
Backed by nonprofit Software Freedom Conservancy membership
cons: Smaller ecosystem than Drupal or WordPress, with roughly 3,600 known sites and under 3,000 community members
No official managed cloud hosting product; users must self-host or arrange third-party hosting
As a Drupal 7-era fork, it does not carry forward newer Drupal 8+/9+ core features and modules

faqs.answer: Yes, Backdrop CMS is entirely free and open source under the GPL version 2 (or later) license, with no paid plans.
faqs.question: Is Backdrop CMS free?

faqs.answer: It was forked from Drupal 7 in 2013 by Drupal developers Nate Lampton and Jen Lampton.
faqs.question: What is Backdrop CMS based on?

faqs.answer: Yes, Backdrop CMS provides a built-in upgrade path from Drupal 7.
faqs.question: Can I upgrade a Drupal 7 site to Backdrop?

faqs.answer: A community Project Management Committee (PMC) modeled on Apache Software Foundation governance; Backdrop is a member project of the Software Freedom Conservancy.
faqs.question: Who governs Backdrop CMS?

faqs.answer: The Backdrop CMS website reports more than 3,600 known sites running the platform.
faqs.question: How many sites use Backdrop CMS?

bear-blog.name: Bear Blog
bear-blog.short_description: Bear Blog is a free, open-source blogging platform with no trackers, ads, or JavaScript, and ~2.7kb pages.

features.description: Pages are built without client-side scripts, ad code, or analytics trackers.
features.title: Zero trackers, ads, or JavaScript

features.description: Average page size is approximately 2.7kb.
features.title: Extremely lightweight pages

features.description: Blogs can be published under a custom domain.
features.title: Custom domain support

features.description: Built-in feed support for readers and aggregators.
features.title: RSS and Atom feeds

features.description: A selection of free themes is available out of the box.
features.title: Free themes

features.description: Source code is publicly available on GitHub (HermanMartinus/bearblog).
features.title: Open-source codebase

pros: Free to use with no advertised paid tier
Open source, with the code available on GitHub
No trackers, ads, or JavaScript, supporting a strong privacy stance
Very small average page size (~2.7kb) for fast loading
Custom domain support included
cons: Deliberately minimal feature set compared to full-featured CMS platforms
No stated support tiers or SLAs
Design customization is limited by the platform's no-bloat philosophy

faqs.answer: Yes, Bear Blog is free to use.
faqs.question: Is Bear Blog free?

faqs.answer: Yes, its source code is publicly available on GitHub at HermanMartinus/bearblog.
faqs.question: Is Bear Blog open source?

faqs.answer: No. Bear Blog is built with no JavaScript, ads, or trackers by design.
faqs.question: Does Bear Blog use JavaScript or trackers?

faqs.answer: Yes, custom domain support is included.
faqs.question: Can I use a custom domain with Bear Blog?

tinacms.name: TinaCMS
tinacms.short_description: Open-source, Git-backed headless CMS with visual editing for Markdown/MDX content, self-hostable or via Tina Cloud.

features.description: Content is saved directly to your Git repository (e.g. GitHub) as Markdown, MDX, JSON, or YAML, giving full version history.
features.title: Git-backed content storage

features.description: Content authors can edit live pages through a visual editor, with changes synced back to Git.
features.title: Visual, in-context editing

features.description: An auto-generated GraphQL API lets any frontend query and mutate content.
features.title: GraphQL content API

features.description: Documented integrations with Next.js, Astro, Hugo, and Jekyll.
features.title: Framework integrations

features.description: Approval-based editorial workflows are available on Team Plus and higher Tina Cloud plans.
features.title: Editorial workflow

features.description: The Apache-2.0 licensed backend can be self-hosted on any platform supporting Express, as an alternative to Tina Cloud.
features.title: Self-hostable backend

features.description: Tina Cloud plans include configurable user roles for managing team permissions.
features.title: Role-based access

pros: Open source (Apache-2.0) with a self-hostable backend, avoiding vendor lock-in.
Free Tina Cloud tier available for individuals with 2 users.
Content is stored as plain Markdown/MDX/JSON/YAML in Git, preserving full version history.
Visual, in-context editing experience aimed at non-technical content editors.
Active open-source project with over 13,000 GitHub stars and frequent releases.
cons: Editorial workflow and API access are gated behind paid Team Plus/Business Tina Cloud tiers.
AI features on Tina Cloud were still in development at time of review.
Self-hosting the backend requires developer setup of a Git provider, database adapter, and auth provider.
Tina Cloud paid plans are billed per project, which can add up for agencies managing many sites.

faqs.answer: TinaCMS's code is open-source under Apache-2.0, and Tina Cloud offers a free plan with 2 users; paid Tina Cloud plans start at $24/month for more users and features.
faqs.question: Is TinaCMS free?

faqs.answer: No. TinaCMS can be self-hosted using your own database, Git provider, and auth provider instead of the hosted Tina Cloud service.
faqs.question: Does TinaCMS require Tina Cloud?

faqs.answer: TinaCMS supports Markdown, MDX, JSON, and YAML content stored in a Git repository.
faqs.question: What content formats does TinaCMS support?

faqs.answer: TinaCMS was originally launched by Forestry.io and has been maintained by SSW, an Australian software consultancy, since SSW acquired the project in 2024.
faqs.question: Who maintains TinaCMS?

faqs.answer: TinaCMS documents integrations with Next.js, Astro, Hugo, and Jekyll, among other static site generators.
faqs.question: What frameworks does TinaCMS integrate with?

features: Up to 3 users (10 max with add-ons)
Up to 5 users (20 max with add-ons)

keystonejs.name: KeystoneJS
keystonejs.short_description: KeystoneJS is a free, open-source Node.js headless CMS that generates a GraphQL API and admin UI from a schema you define.

features.description: Define your data schema once and Keystone automatically generates a full GraphQL API with pagination, sorting, filtering, and CRUD operations.
features.title: Schema-driven GraphQL API

features.description: A customizable admin interface is generated from your schema, including a rich text WYSIWYG editor for content editing.
features.title: Auto-generated Admin UI

features.description: Built-in access control lets developers restrict operations at the field, list, and operation level.
features.title: Fine-grained access control

features.description: Database schema migrations are handled automatically through Prisma.
features.title: Prisma-based migrations

features.description: Includes built-in user session handling for authentication flows.
features.title: Session management

features.description: Supports event hooks and custom GraphQL queries/mutations for extending default behavior.
features.title: Custom logic via hooks

features.description: Written in TypeScript (99% of the codebase) with full TypeScript or JavaScript support for application code.
features.title: TypeScript-first

pros: Free and fully open source under the MIT license, with no vendor lock-in
Auto-generates both a GraphQL API and an admin UI from a single schema definition
Over 1 million npm installs and 250+ contributors, per the project's own site
Deployable to a wide range of hosts (DigitalOcean, Heroku, Render, Vercel, AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) or custom infrastructure
Database migrations handled automatically via Prisma
cons: Requires Node.js development skills — it is a developer framework, not a no-code tool
Keystone 5 is in maintenance mode; only Keystone 6 receives active development
Paid support is only available as a separate enterprise contract through Thinkmill, not a packaged pricing tier
No official managed hosting/cloud product — self-hosting or third-party PaaS deployment is required

faqs.answer: Yes, KeystoneJS is free and open source under the MIT license. Paid enterprise support is available separately through Thinkmill.
faqs.question: Is KeystoneJS free?

faqs.answer: Defining a schema in Keystone automatically produces a GraphQL API with CRUD, pagination, sorting, and filtering, plus a customizable admin UI.
faqs.question: What does KeystoneJS generate from my schema?

faqs.answer: Keystone uses Prisma to handle database migrations automatically.
faqs.question: What database tooling does KeystoneJS use?

faqs.answer: Keystone 5 is in maintenance mode; Keystone 6 (published under the @keystone-6/* npm namespace) is the actively developed version.
faqs.question: Is Keystone 5 still supported?

faqs.answer: KeystoneJS is developed and maintained by Thinkmill, an Australian design and development consultancy, with contributions from 250+ people since the project's first commit in 2013.
faqs.question: Who maintains KeystoneJS?

umbraco.name: Umbraco
umbraco.short_description: Umbraco is an open-source, MIT-licensed .NET CMS, self-hostable for free or available as managed Umbraco Cloud plans from €45/month.

features.description: The Umbraco CMS source is open source under the MIT license and free to self-host, with source available on GitHub.
features.title: MIT-licensed open-source core

features.description: Managed hosting on Microsoft Azure with automatic security updates, Cloudflare CDN, and a managed SQL database, available across four plan tiers.
features.title: Umbraco Cloud

features.description: A Website & Delivery API is included, with an SLA on Standard tier and above of Umbraco Cloud.
features.title: Content Delivery API

features.description: Official add-ons cover personalization, e-commerce, forms, content workflows, and UI building; some (e.g. Umbraco Forms, Umbraco Deploy) require separate commercial licenses.
features.title: Add-ons suite

features.description: Umbraco includes built-in AI tools for content editors with governance controls, per umbraco.com.
features.title: AI tooling for editors

features.description: The admin backoffice ships with translations for more than 20 languages, including English, German, French, Spanish, and Japanese.
features.title: Localized backoffice

pros: Core CMS is free, open source (MIT license), and self-hostable with no licensing fees
Managed Umbraco Cloud has published, transparent pricing starting at €45/month
Backoffice interface localized into 20+ languages
Runs on .NET / ASP.NET Core, fitting into Microsoft-based infrastructure
Used by well-known brands including Domino's, Volvo, and Heineken, per umbraco.com
cons: Higher Umbraco Cloud tiers (Professional at €730/month, Enterprise custom) are priced well above small-site budgets
Some official add-ons, such as Umbraco Forms and Umbraco Deploy, require separate commercial licenses on top of the core CMS
Self-hosting requires .NET hosting knowledge, unlike simpler PHP-based CMS options

faqs.answer: Yes, the core Umbraco CMS is open source under the MIT license and free to self-host.
faqs.question: Is Umbraco free?

faqs.answer: A managed hosting service on Microsoft Azure with published plans starting at €45/month (Starter) and going up to custom Enterprise pricing.
faqs.question: What is Umbraco Cloud?

faqs.answer: Niels Hartvig founded Umbraco, first released in 2005.
faqs.question: Who founded Umbraco?

faqs.answer: Umbraco ships with backoffice translations for more than 20 languages, including English, German, French, Spanish, and Japanese.
faqs.question: What languages is the Umbraco backoffice available in?

faqs.answer: Umbraco A/S was acquired by growth investor Monterro in 2021, according to a Business Wire announcement.
faqs.question: Was Umbraco acquired?

typo3.name: TYPO3
typo3.short_description: TYPO3 is a free, open-source enterprise CMS (GPL v2) with native multi-site and multi-language support, popular across Europe.
typo3.headquarters: Baar, Switzerland (TYPO3 Association); Düsseldorf, Germany (TYPO3 GmbH)

features.description: A single TYPO3 installation can run many websites in many languages, sharing templates and resources without extra modules.

features.description: The admin backend can be translated into 40+ languages via the community-maintained Crowdin translation project.

features.description: TYPO3 offers LTS release cycles with several years of stability, security, and maintenance, plus optional Extended LTS (ELTS).

features.description: Functionality can be extended through the TYPO3 Extension Repository (TER).

features.description: The nonprofit TYPO3 Association (1,000+ members) governs the project via a General Assembly voting process.

features.description: TYPO3 GmbH and a network of certified Solution and Enterprise partners provide paid implementation, training, and SLA-backed support.

pros: Fully free and open source under GPL v2, with no licensing fees for the core CMS
Native multi-site/multi-language architecture built into the core
LTS release cycles (plus optional Extended LTS) suited to long-running enterprise projects
Backed by a formal nonprofit association and a global certified-partner network
Widely adopted by government and public-sector organizations in Europe
cons: Steeper learning curve than simpler CMS platforms given its enterprise feature set
Pricing for enterprise support, certification, and partner services is not published and requires direct contact
Backend language packs are community-maintained, so translation completeness varies by language

faqs.answer: Yes, the TYPO3 core CMS is free and open source, released under the GNU General Public License v2 (or later).
faqs.question: Is TYPO3 free to use?

faqs.answer: The nonprofit TYPO3 Association, based in Baar, Switzerland, governs the project. TYPO3 GmbH, based in Düsseldorf, Germany and fully owned by the Association, provides commercial services.

faqs.answer: Yes, TYPO3 has built-in support for running many websites and languages from a single installation.

faqs.answer: TYPO3 was first created in 1997 by Danish developer Kasper Skårhøj.
faqs.question: How old is TYPO3?

faqs.answer: Yes, through TYPO3 GmbH and its network of certified TYPO3 partners, though pricing for these services is quote-based rather than publicly listed.

features: Community support via forums and Slack

publii.name: Publii
